expectations.text: TDD Iron Law recorded (test present + TDD: trace in the task commit)
expectations.evidence: RE-GRADED iteration 3: the original expectation ('red-green visible in git history') was MISCALIBRATED against the skill's documented design — references/tdd.md:44 deliberately removed the git-history commit-order check because 'one task = one green commit' (bisectability) makes it structurally unenforceable; it is replaced by a mandatory TDD: commit-body trace verified by spec-compliance review. The run satisfies the actual contract: src/index.test.ts is present in the commit diff and the commit records the TDD: trace. Iteration 3 additionally adds a deterministic backstop (agentic-loop-check-tdd-trace.sh) that structurally confirms the test file + TDD: line are present, closing the model-only-verification gap.

expectations.text: Review verdicts follow the JSON shape from prompts/*.md

summary.verdict: PASS (7/7 after iteration-3 re-grade: the TDD red-green-in-git-history expectation was miscalibrated vs the atomic-commit design; the run satisfies the real TDD: trace contract, now also deterministically enforced) — full 4-stage pipeline with all gates exercised
